title: SEC3: notify callback fires JS call with discarded Result
description: transport.rs line 115-118: the notification path calls `f.call1(...).map(...)` but the result of `Option::map` is discarded (the outer expression is just a statement). The inner `f.call1()` returns `Result<JsValue, JsValue>`; if the JS callback throws, the error is silently swallowed with no log. Either log the error (`if let Err(e) = f.call1(...) { console::warn(...) }`) or accept the discard explicitly with `let _ = f.call1(...)`. The current `.map(...)` form looks intentional but achieves nothing and will confuse a reader.
